Question: If a shopkeeper marks his goods 40% above the cost price and allows a discount of 25% on the marked price, then his profit or loss percentage is:
The ratio of two numbers is 3 : 4 and their HCF is 4. Find their LCM.
A and B can complete a piece of work in 15 days and 10 days respectively. They started working together, but A left after 2 days. In how many days will B complete the remaining work?
A train 150 meters long passes a telegraph post in 12 seconds. Find the speed of the train in km/h.
If x + 1/x = 5, then the value of x² + 1/x² is:
The average score of a batsman in 15 innings is 40. He scores 85 runs in his 16th inning. What is his new average score?
In a triangle ABC, if ∠A = 70° and the internal bisectors of ∠B and ∠C meet at point O, then find the value of ∠BOC.
If sin θ + cos θ = √2 cos θ, then the value of cos θ - sin θ is:
A sum of money becomes 3 times of itself in 5 years at a certain rate of simple interest. In how many years will it become 9 times of itself at the same rate?
The single discount equivalent to a successive discount series of 20%, 10% and 5% is:
The population of a town increases by 10% annually. If its present population is 20,000, what will be its population after 2 years?

Two pipes can fill a cistern in 12 minutes and 15 minutes respectively. An empty pipe can drain it in 20 minutes. If all three pipes are opened together, in how many minutes will the cistern be completely filled?
A boat goes 12 km downstream and comes back to the starting point in 3 hours. If the speed of the current is 3 km/h, find the speed of the boat in still water.
If a + b + c = 6 and a² + b² + c² = 14, then find the value of ab + bc + ca.
The ratio of the income of two persons is 9 : 7 and the ratio of their expenditures is 4 : 3. If each of them saves ₹2000 per month, find the income of the first person.
The area of a circle is 616 cm². Find its perimeter/circumference. (Take π = 22/7)
If sec 4A = cosec (A - 20°), where 4A is an acute angle, find the value of A.
What is the compound interest on ₹10,000 for 2 years at 10% per annum, compounded annually?
A shopkeeper mixes two variants of rice costing ₹60 per kg and ₹85 per kg in a certain ratio. If the mixture is worth ₹75 per kg, find the ratio in which they were mixed.
When a number is divided by 56, the remainder obtained is 29. What will be the remainder when the same number is divided by 8?
If 15% of A is equal to 20% of B, then A : B is equal to:
By selling an article for ₹720, a man loses 10%. At what price should he sell it to gain 5%?
A man crosses a 600m long street in 5 minutes. What is his speed in km/h?
If x³ + y³ = 35 and x + y = 5, find the value of x * y.
The sum of the ages of a father and his son is 45 years. Five years ago, the product of their ages was 34. What is the father's current age?
The length of the chord of a circle is 16 cm and its distance from the center is 6 cm. Find the radius of the circle.
Evaluate: tan 1° tan 2° tan 3° ... tan 89°
If a certain sum amounts to ₹12100 in 2 years and ₹13310 in 3 years under compound interest compounded annually, find the rate of interest per annum.
The side of an equilateral triangle is 8 cm. Find its area.

A container contains 40 liters of milk. From this container, 4 liters of milk was taken out and replaced by water. This process was repeated further two times. How much milk is now contained by the container?
If A's salary is 25% more than B's salary, then by how much percentage is B's salary less than A's salary?
A dealer sells an article at a profit of 20%. If he had bought it for 10% less and sold it for ₹18 less, he would have gained 30%. Find the cost price of the article.
If a person walks at 14 km/h instead of 10 km/h, he would have walked 20 km more. What is the actual distance traveled by him at 10 km/h?
If x = √3 + √2, then the value of x - 1/x is:
The mean of 5 observations is 60. If the mean of the first 3 observations is 57 and that of the last 3 observations is 62, find the third observation.
In a circle with center O, an arc AB subtends an angle of 140° at the center. Find the value of angle ACB, where C is a point on the major arc.
If cos θ = 4/5, then the value of (tan θ + sin θ) / (1 + cos θ) is:
Find the length of the longest rod that can be placed in a room 12m long, 9m broad, and 8m high.
If 7-digit number 5432y4x is completely divisible by 9, and the number is even, find the maximum possible value of x + y.
